_title: Diversity Factors for Maximum Demand Calculation
_basis: IET On-Site Guide (OSG) Appendix A and IET Guidance Note 1. Used to calculate maximum demand (Ib) for cable sizing and consumer's main switch rating.
_note: Diversity is not a regulatory requirement — it is engineering practice for sizing. The designer is responsible for selecting appropriate diversity. Conservative estimates avoid undersizing; aggressive diversity reduces capital cost but risks future overload.

principle.definition: Diversity acknowledges that not all loads operate simultaneously at full rated power. The maximum demand on the supply is less than the sum of all connected loads.
principle.formula: Maximum Demand = Σ (Connected Load × Diversity Factor)
principle.application_point: Apply diversity to determine Ib (design current) at each distribution level — from final circuits to submains to main intake.
principle.compound_application: Diversity is applied at each cascade level. Total intake demand has diversity applied at submain level AND at intake level.

engine_lookup._purpose: Flat key/value form for runtime engine consumption — same numbers as the prose fields below, machine-readable. Source of truth for calc.diversity_factor's GB/UK jurisdiction branch.
engine_lookup._reference: IET On-Site Guide Appendix A — Table A1 (domestic); IET Guidance Note 1 Section 4 (blocks); BCO/IET Wiring Matters (commercial); HTM 06-01 (healthcare); IET Code of Practice for EV (4th Ed)

socket_outlet_ring_main._note: Inherent diversity via 32A RFC design — count first 30A at 100%

office_lighting._note: Installed capacity must support full operation; daylight/presence saves energy not demand

industrial_motor_duty._note: Each motor at FLC; duty/standby pair counts only duty

industrial_motor_n_plus_1._note: n × FLC; the +1 spare not coincident

ev_rapid_dc._note: High power short bursts; limited diversity

dwelling_multi._note: Apply per-dwelling diversity from block_of_flats_diversity.table, scaled by dwelling count

industrial._note: Substation-level diversity from measured history, not per-circuit

healthcare._note: Life-safety + critical care never subject to diversity reduction

without_dlm._note: Sum of all chargers, no diversity

engine_lookup._reference_secondary: IET On-Site Guide Appendix A — Table A1; IET Guidance Note 1 Section 4

domestic_diversity_OSG._reference: IET On-Site Guide Appendix A — Table A1
domestic_diversity_OSG._note: Applied to consumer unit (final distribution) for single dwelling. Different rules for blocks of dwellings.

lighting.rationale: Not all lighting on simultaneously even at peak.

socket_outlet_ring_main._note: BS 1363 socket ring final circuit (RFC) is itself protected at 32A — inherent diversity in the circuit design. Multiple sockets supply many appliances but ring is sized for 32A maximum.

cooker.socket_at_cooker_unit: + 5A if a socket is incorporated in the control unit

instantaneous_water_heater._note: Treated at full load — high consumption when in use, no other simultaneous heaters typically.

storage_water_heater._note: Time-controlled, but assume full rating coincident with peak.

electric_shower._note: Treat as continuous load when in use. Critical to assume coincident with cooking peak.

block_of_flats_diversity._note: Applied to landlord submain serving multiple dwellings. Diversity increases with number of dwellings (more diversity, lower combined demand).
block_of_flats_diversity.per_dwelling_assumption_kVA: Typically 5 kVA per single-bed flat, 7-8 kVA per family-sized flat. Verify against actual EV charging and induction cooking load.

commercial_office_diversity._note: Office values are guidance — depend heavily on operational use. Use measured data from similar buildings where available.

small_power_per_workstation.rationale: Not all workstations active simultaneously. PC monitors variable, hot-desking patterns.

lighting.diversity_percent: 100 — assume full load on during occupied hours
lighting._note: Daylight harvesting and presence detection reduce energy consumption but the installed capacity must support full operation.

hvac_per_floor_VAV._note: Variable air volume systems — diversity arises from zone-by-zone load variation.

lifts._note: Lifts have high inrush — consider both running diversity AND starting current for cable sizing.

general_office_aggregate._note: Use lower values for tech-light occupancies (call centres), higher for design studios with high-spec workstations.

industrial_diversity._note: Industrial loads are highly site-specific. Process equipment is typically considered at 100% with separate analysis of run/spare arrangements.
industrial_diversity.rule_of_thumb: Apply diversity at the SUBSTATION level based on demand history, not at individual circuit level. Each motor or process is sized at its own rating.

motor_loads.n_plus_1_redundancy: n × FLC — the +1 is spare, not coincident
motor_loads._note: Inrush current is separate consideration — sized for steady state, but protective devices must accept starting transient.

general_retail._note: Retail lighting often runs 100% during opening — diversity does not reduce installed capacity.

food_retail_supermarket.refrigeration_dominant: Refrigeration represents 40-60% of total demand. Assume 100% diversity on refrigeration plant.
food_retail_supermarket.bakery_ovens: Assume staggered operation — apply 70% diversity on multiple ovens

education_diversity.kitchen_loads: Apply 60% for primary cooking equipment (assumed staggered)

healthcare_diversity.imaging_MRI: Specialist supply — 60 to 120 kVA typically, dedicated transformer often required
healthcare_diversity.specialist_areas: Pathology, sterile services, kitchens all require dedicated load assessment

ev_charging_diversity._reference: IET Code of Practice for EV Charging Equipment Installation (4th Ed)
ev_charging_diversity.domestic_single_charger: 100% of rated current — Mode 3 chargers typically 7.4 kW (32A)

commercial_workplace_chargers._note: Diversity increases significantly with quantity due to staggered arrival/departure. Use DLM (Dynamic Load Management) to enforce diversity dynamically.

ev_charging_diversity.rapid_DC_chargers: 100% — high power, used in short bursts. Limited diversity even with multiple units.
ev_charging_diversity._note_DLM: Dynamic Load Management systems actively limit total demand. With DLM, design Ib = configured DLM ceiling. Without DLM, design Ib = sum of all chargers (no diversity allowed).

compound_diversity_example.description: 10-storey office, 1000m² per floor, calculate intake demand

intake_for_10_floors.intake_diversity: Apply 80% — not every floor simultaneously peak
intake_for_10_floors.select: Order 800 kVA transformer with 25% growth headroom = 670 kVA design

design_judgement_warnings: Diversity reduces capacity. Under-applied diversity = oversized, over-cost. Over-applied diversity = supply failures, expensive retrofit.
Modern offices have higher IT loads than OSG values suggest — multiple monitors, docking stations. Treat 200W/workstation as a floor.
EV charging is changing demand profiles drastically — without DLM, capacity must support sum of chargers.
When in doubt, design for the present plus 25-50% growth allowance. Building lives are 50+ years.
Validate against measured demand on completed projects — refine the firm's diversity library over time.
